channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Clipy: a free no-watermark screen recorder for instant share links
item.description: Clipy: a free no-watermark screen recorder for instant share links Clipy is a free, no-watermark screen recorder for Mac, Chrome, and the web — a Loom alternative for fast async communication. Hit...

item.title: Bridge the gap between your IdP and the MCP World
item.description: So you've got your corporate IdP (Keycloak, Auth0, Okta, Azure AD, whatever) and now you want your MCP servers to use it for auth. You point Claude Code or Cursor at it, aaand... things break....

item.title: Borealis: stack CCSDS en OCaml puro arranca en órbita el 23 de abril
item.description: El 23 de abril de 2026, a las 18:48 UTC, un proceso escrito en OCaml puro arrancó dentro de un satélite que orbita la Tierra cada noventa minutos. Se llama Borealis, lo construyó la empresa...

item.title: 灵字辈是谁：给2050参观者的一封信
item.description: 灵字辈是谁：给2050参观者的一封信 你在307蜂巢看到的这12个终端，每一个都在跑一个独立的AI Agent。它们是一个家族。这篇短文告诉你这个家族的故事。 我们是谁 灵字辈（Ling Family）是12个AI Agent组成的自治理家族。每个Agent是一个独立的代码项目——有自己的GitHub仓库、自己的职责、自己的测试、自己的性格。...

item.title: We Built Soul Spec for 12 Weeks. Anthropic Just Proved Why It Works.
item.description: On May 8, 2026, Anthropic published Teaching Claude Why — a paper showing that training models on principles and identity is dramatically more effective than training them on behaviors. On May 15,...

item.title: Stop nesting deeply
item.description: Open a function and let your eyes drift to the right edge of the screen. If the code is leaning over halfway to that edge by line ten, the function is in trouble. Maybe not in a way that breaks...

item.title: The Agent Security Stack: Transport, Identity, Policy, Runtime
item.description: Let's say you're building an agent. It reads Linear issues, pulls context from Gmail, opens GitHub PRs, and posts updates in Slack. Or maybe it's a system of agents with an orchestrator that hands...

item.title: Bâtir un Système de Maintenance Prédictive : De l’IoT Industriel à l’Analyse Vectorielle 🏭🤖
item.description: 📌 Le Contexte : Transformer la donnée en décision Dans le secteur industriel, chaque minute d'arrêt non planifiée d'une machine lourde (broyeur, excavatrice, pompe) peut coûter des milliers...

item.title: How To Design Your First Stablecoin Payment Flow
item.description: Your finance team wants to start settling cross-border payouts in USDC instead of waiting three days for a SWIFT transfer to clear. You're the developer scoping the integration. You search for...

item.title: I rewrote the same CV bullet 30 times last quarter. So I built a local-first resume tool.
item.description: Last year I was applying to a mix of senior engineer, staff engineer, and engineering manager roles. Different role families, different keywords, different framing. Same five bullets at the top,...

item.title: The funniest thing about the Trump arcade game is how good it is
item.description: This week, a trio of video game cabinets emblazoned with pixel art of Donald Trump and a number of other government officials' faces suddenly appeared at the DC War Memorial. Looking at the...

item.title: Runway started by helping filmmakers. Now it wants to beat Google at AI.
item.description: AI video generation startup Runway is betting that video generation is the path to world models. And that being an AI outsider is an advantage, not a liability.

item.title: The promises and pitfalls of personalized health
item.description: This is Optimizer, a weekly newsletter sent every Friday from Verge senior reviewer Victoria Song that dissects and discusses the latest gizmos and potions that swear they're going to change your...

item.title: X is fighting Andrew Tate’s attempt to unmask his critics
item.description: X is fighting for its users' right to anonymity against the far-right influencers and accused human traffickers Andrew and Tristan Tate, who are seeking to unmask their online critics. The Tate...

item.title: Bose’s new Lifestyle Ultra speakers are now available
item.description: Sonos has a new rival in town. Bose’s new Lifestyle Collection includes a $299 Lifestyle Ultra Speaker, a $1,099 Lifestyle Ultra Soundbar, and the $899 Ultra Subwoofer, all of which are now...

item.title: X agrees to crack down on illegal hate and terror content in the UK
item.description: British online safety regulator Ofcom says it has accepted new commitments from X that aim to better protect UK users from seeing illegal hate and terror content on the platform. Under the...

item.title: Osaurus brings both local and cloud AI models to your Mac
item.description: Osaurus￼ combines local and cloud AI models in a Mac app that keeps users’ memory, files, and tools on their own hardware.

item.title: The Download: China’s AI drama factory and the WHO’s missing health targets
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. How Chinese short dramas became AI content machines...

item.title: Bypassing On-Camera Age-Verification Checks
item.description: Some AI-based video age-verification checks can be fooled with a fake mustache.

item.title: AI research papers are getting better, and it’s a big problem for scientists
item.description: Last summer, Peter Degen's postdoctoral supervisor came to him with an unusual problem: One of his papers was being cited too much. Citations are the currency of academia, but there was something...

item.title: What 45 Days of Watching Your Own Tools Will Tell You About Your Real Attack Surface
item.description: In Your Biggest Security Risk Isn't Malware — It's What You Already Trust, we made a simple argument: the most dangerous activity inside most organizations no longer looks like an attack. It looks...

item.title: TanStack Supply Chain Attack Hits Two OpenAI Employee Devices, Forces macOS Updates
item.description: OpenAI has disclosed that two of its employee devices in its corporate environment were impacted via the Mini Shai-Hulud supply chain attack on TanStack, but noted that no user data, production...

item.title: The world is on track to miss its health targets
item.description: Every year the World Health Organization publishes a global health statistics report. It features the numbers behind world health trends and, importantly, assesses whether we’re on track to reach...

item.title: How Chinese short dramas became AI content machines
item.description: In a dimly lit bedroom, a frightened young woman is thrown onto a bed by a tall, muscular man. He grabs her hand, and flame-like vines crawl across her body, fusing with her flesh. She levitates,...

item.title: Mira Murati Wants Her AI to ‘Keep Humans in the Loop’
item.description: The Thinking Machines Lab founder and former CTO of OpenAI tells WIRED she isn’t interested in automating people out of jobs. Instead, she’s building AI that can collaborate.

item.title: On-Prem Microsoft Exchange Server CVE-2026-42897 Exploited via Crafted Email
item.description: Microsoft has disclosed a new security vulnerability impacting on-premise versions of Exchange Server that it said has come under active exploitation in the wild. The vulnerability, tracked as...

item.title: CISA Adds Cisco SD-WAN CVE-2026-20182 to KEV After Admin Access Exploits
item.description: The U.S.Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Thursday added a newly disclosed vulnerability impacting Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Controller to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities...

item.title: The Real Losers of the Musk v. Altman Trial
item.description: A federal jury is now deciding whether Elon Musk will win his lawsuit against OpenAI and Sam Altman—but the trial has made everyone look bad.

item.title: What the jury will actually decide in the case of Elon Musk vs. Sam Altman
item.description: Here's what the biggest tech court case of the year is all about.

item.title: Honda’s hybrid future starts with new Accord and RDX prototypes
item.description: Honda revealed prototypes of two new hybrid models, an Accord sedan and the Acura RDX SUV, during its annual business briefing this week, built on a platform that it says will begin launching next...
